name: Force Majeure
text: Neither Party shall be liable for failure to perform its obligations under this Agreement if such failure is due to events beyond its reasonable control including natural disasters, war, strikes, or government actions.

legal_basis.note: Agreement to do impossible act — frustration doctrine
Perishing of goods — specific goods contracts

invalid_if: Force majeure events defined too broadly
source: Indian Contract Act, 1872 S.32 and S.56
title: Force Majeure
dispute_hotspots: COVID-19 as FM — Energy Watchdog v CERC: price increase alone is not FM
Government action — fine line between FM and commercial risk allocation
Payment still due for work completed before FM — often disputed
Foreseeability — if event was foreseeable, FM defence fails
